concept_name: Magnetic Field of a Long Straight Current-Carrying Wire (Right-Hand Rule)
source_book: DC Pandey Class 12 Ch.4 Moving Charges and Magnetism §4.4 (Magnetic field due to a current element / long straight wire) — table-of-contents reference only; teaching authored from first principles. Phase 0 validation demo Sim 3 (session 60, Day 1). The atomic concept being taught is the *direction* and *magnitude* of B around a long straight current-carrying wire: B forms concentric circles in planes perpendicular to the wire, direction by right-hand rule (thumb along I, fingers curl B), magnitude B = μ₀I/(2πr). This is the most-confused concept in Class 12 magnetism: students think B points along I, conflate × / · with current direction, and try to do the right-hand rule on flat paper. Three.js makes the 3D-ness visible.

real_world_anchor.primary: Picture a USB cable plugged into a power bank charging your phone. A small steady current flows in the wire. Hold a magnetic compass within a few centimeters of the wire. Watch the needle swing — it does NOT point to magnetic north anymore; it tilts perpendicular to the wire. Switch off the current — the needle snaps back. The wire creates a magnetic field that wraps around it in 3D circles, strong enough to overpower Earth's field at close range.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Picture an overhead high-tension power line above a Delhi street. Hundreds of amperes flow through it. Standing directly below with a sensitive compass, you would see the needle deflect perceptibly even from several meters away — because B falls off only as 1/r, not 1/r², and the current is enormous.
real_world_anchor.tertiary: Picture an MRI machine's main coil — a current-carrying tube with the patient inside. The tube creates a magnetic field along its axis (this is actually a solenoid, but each turn is a loop, and each tiny segment of each loop is a tiny straight current-carrying piece). The whole field of the machine is just countless straight-wire B-fields added up. Master the single straight wire, you understand every magnet in the world.

r.name: Perpendicular distance from wire to field point

formulas.direction: B is tangent to the circle of radius r centered on the wire, in the plane perpendicular to the wire at the field point
formulas.right_hand_rule: Point right thumb along current I; curled fingers give the sense (clockwise/counter-clockwise) of B around the wire

constraints: Wire is treated as infinitely long and infinitesimally thin (idealization holds when r << wire length and r >> wire diameter)
B-field at any point depends ONLY on the perpendicular distance r from the wire — not on position along the wire
B is everywhere perpendicular to the radial direction (tangent to the circle through the point)
B has zero radial component and zero axial component — purely azimuthal in cylindrical coordinates around the wire
Direction reverses if the current direction reverses (right-hand rule respects sign of I)

aha_moment.statement: B forms 3D circles around the wire — never along it.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: Drag the camera around the wire; circles look the same shape from every angle, only the perspective changes.

STATE_1.title: Oersted's Hook — A Current Deflects a Compass

scene_composition.text: Straight wire — current OFF
1820: Hans Christian Oersted

compass needle
(imagine it nearby)

tts_sentences.text_en: Look at the scene: a vertical wire and a compass beside it. Right now the needle points north — its red tip toward you.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दृश्य देखिए: एक vertical wire और उसके बगल में एक compass। अभी needle north की तरफ point कर रही है — इसकी लाल tip आपकी तरफ है।
tts_sentences.text_te: దృశ్యాన్ని చూడండి: ఒక vertical wire మరియు దాని పక్కన ఒక compass. ఇప్పుడు needle north వైపు point చేస్తోంది — దాని ఎర్రటి tip మీ వైపు ఉంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch what happens when current starts flowing through the wire — wait for it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ध्यान से देखिए — जब wire में current बहना शुरू होती है, तब क्या होता है — ज़रा रुकिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: జాగ్రత్తగా చూడండి — wire లో current ప్రవహించడం మొదలైనప్పుడు ఏం జరుగుతుందో — కొంచెం ఆగండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: There — the compass needle just SWUNG. It snapped to point perpendicular to the wire, no longer north.
tts_sentences.text_hi: देखिए — compass needle अभी अचानक हिल गई! यह wire के perpendicular direction में snap हो गई, अब north की तरफ नहीं रही।
tts_sentences.text_te: చూడండి — compass needle ఇప్పుడు ఒక్కసారిగా కదిలింది! అది wire కి perpendicular గా snap అయింది, ఇక north వైపు లేదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Hans Christian Oersted discovered this in 1820. A current-carrying wire produces a magnetic field. But where is it, and which way does it point?
tts_sentences.text_hi: Hans Christian Oersted ने 1820 में यह खोज की। एक current-carrying wire, magnetic field produce करती है। लेकिन यह field कहाँ है, और किस direction में point करती है?
tts_sentences.text_te: Hans Christian Oersted 1820 లో దీన్ని కనుగొన్నాడు. ఒక current-carrying wire, magnetic field ను produce చేస్తుంది. కానీ అది ఎక్కడ ఉంది, మరియు ఏ direction లో point చేస్తుంది?

STATE_2.title: The Magnetic Field — 3D Circles Around the Wire

scene_composition.text: Magnetic field — full 3D
Drag to rotate. Field circles loop around the wire at every height.
I (current direction)

tts_sentences.text_en: With current flowing, the wire produces a magnetic field that fills the space around it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: current बहने के साथ, wire अपने चारों ओर के space में एक magnetic field produce करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: current ప్రవహించడంతో, wire తన చుట్టూ ఉన్న space లో ఒక magnetic field ను produce చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: But here is the surprise: the field does NOT point along the wire. And it does NOT point radially away from the wire either.
tts_sentences.text_hi: लेकिन यहाँ एक हैरान करने वाली बात है: यह field wire के along point नहीं करती। और यह wire से radially बाहर की तरफ भी point नहीं करती।
tts_sentences.text_te: కానీ ఇక్కడ ఒక ఆశ్చర్యకరమైన విషయం ఉంది: ఈ field, wire వెంట point చేయదు. మరియు ఇది wire నుండి radially బయటకు కూడా point చేయదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Drag the camera to rotate the view. You see the field forms perfect circles that loop around the wire.
tts_sentences.text_hi: camera को rotate करने के लिए drag कीजिए। आप देख सकते हैं कि field, perfect circles बनाती है जो wire के चारों ओर loop करती हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: view ని rotate చేయడానికి camera ని drag చేయండి. మీరు చూడవచ్చు — field, perfect circles గా ఏర్పడుతుంది, అవి wire చుట్టూ loop అవుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: These are not 2D circles on paper. They are real 3D circles in three-dimensional space — at every height along the wire, a stack of circles.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ये कागज़ पर बनाए गए 2D circles नहीं हैं। ये असली 3D circles हैं, three-dimensional space में — wire के हर height पर, circles का एक stack।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇవి కాగితం మీద గీసిన 2D circles కాదు. ఇవి నిజమైన 3D circles, three-dimensional space లో ఉన్నాయి — wire వెంట ప్రతి height వద్ద, circles యొక్క ఒక stack.

misconception_watch.belief: B points in the same direction as I (along the wire)
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Rotate the camera; observe that the field arrows are tangent to circles, never parallel to the wire.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: B is perpendicular to the wire at every point — it circles around, never along.

STATE_3.title: Case A — Current UP, B counter-clockwise

scene_composition.text: Case A — Current UP
I points UP — yellow dots flow up the wire

B circles run counter-clockwise
(viewed from above)

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the yellow dots flowing UP the wire. That is the direction of the current — the conventional current I.
tts_sentences.text_hi: wire में ऊपर की तरफ बह रहे पीले dots को देखिए। यही conventional current I की direction है।
tts_sentences.text_te: wire లో పైకి ప్రవహిస్తున్న పసుపు dots ని చూడండి. అదే conventional current I యొక్క direction.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now look at the green B-field rings around the wire. They are rotating — counter-clockwise as seen from above.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब wire के चारों ओर बने हरे B-field rings को देखिए। वे rotate हो रहे हैं — ऊपर से देखने पर counter-clockwise।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు wire చుట్టూ ఉన్న ఆకుపచ్చ B-field rings ని చూడండి. అవి rotate అవుతున్నాయి — పైనుండి చూస్తే counter-clockwise గా.

tts_sentences.text_en: Bring up your right hand. Point the thumb UP, along the current. See how your fingers naturally curl counter-clockwise — same as the rings.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अपना right hand उठाइए। thumb को ऊपर की तरफ point कीजिए, current की direction में। देखिए कैसे आपकी fingers naturally counter-clockwise curl होती हैं — बिल्कुल उन rings की तरह।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీ right hand తీసుకోండి. thumb ని పైకి point చేయండి, current వెంట. మీ fingers naturally counter-clockwise గా curl అవడం చూడండి — rings లాగే.

tts_sentences.text_en: This is the right-hand rule for Case A: thumb along I, fingers curl in the direction of B. Always the RIGHT hand, never the left.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह है Case A के लिए right-hand rule: thumb को I के along रखो, fingers B की direction में curl होती हैं। हमेशा RIGHT hand, कभी left नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది Case A కోసం right-hand rule: thumb ని I వెంట పెట్టండి, fingers B యొక్క direction లో curl అవుతాయి. ఎప్పుడూ RIGHT hand మాత్రమే, ఎప్పటికీ left కాదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Hold this case in your head. Next state: we flip the current downward and watch the field reverse.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इस case को अपने दिमाग में रखिए। अगला state: हम current को नीचे की तरफ flip करेंगे और field को reverse होते हुए देखेंगे।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఈ case ని మీ మనసులో పెట్టుకోండి. తదుపరి state: మనం current ని కిందికి flip చేసి, field reverse అవడాన్ని చూస్తాం.

misconception_watch.belief: Use the left hand because that is the natural one to wrap
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Always RIGHT hand for B around current — the convention is fixed by the cross-product right-hand rule, never the left.

STATE_4.title: Case B — Current DOWN, B clockwise

scene_composition.text: Case B — Current DOWN
I points DOWN — yellow dots flow down the wire

B circles run clockwise
(viewed from above)

tts_sentences.text_en: Now we reverse the battery. Watch the yellow dots — they are flowing DOWN the wire. The current direction has flipped.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब हम battery को reverse करते हैं। yellow dots को देखिए — वे wire के अंदर नीचे की तरफ flow हो रहे हैं। current की direction flip हो गई है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మనం battery ని reverse చేస్తున్నాం. yellow dots చూడండి — అవి wire లో కిందికి flow అవుతున్నాయి. current direction flip అయింది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Look at the rings. They are still circling the wire, but the rotation has reversed. They now go CLOCKWISE — viewed from above.
tts_sentences.text_hi: rings को देखिए। वे अभी भी wire के चारों तरफ circle कर रही हैं, लेकिन rotation reverse हो गई है। अब वे CLOCKWISE जा रही हैं — ऊपर से देखने पर।
tts_sentences.text_te: rings చూడండి. అవి ఇంకా wire చుట్టూ circling చేస్తున్నాయి, కానీ rotation reverse అయింది. అవి ఇప్పుడు CLOCKWISE గా వెళ్తున్నాయి — పై నుండి చూస్తే.

tts_sentences.text_en: Bring up your right hand again. This time point the thumb DOWN, along the new current. Your fingers curl in the opposite sense — clockwise.
tts_sentences.text_hi: फिर से अपना right hand उठाइए। इस बार thumb को नीचे की तरफ point कीजिए, नई current की direction में। आपकी fingers opposite sense में curl होंगी — clockwise।
tts_sentences.text_te: మళ్ళీ మీ right hand తీసుకోండి. ఈసారి thumb ని కిందికి point చేయండి, కొత్త current వెంట. మీ fingers opposite sense లో curl అవుతాయి — clockwise గా.

tts_sentences.text_en: Same right hand, opposite thumb, opposite curl. Reverse the current and the entire B-field reverses with it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Same right hand, opposite thumb, opposite curl। current को reverse करो और पूरा B-field उसके साथ reverse हो जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: అదే right hand, opposite thumb, opposite curl. current ని reverse చేయండి మరియు మొత్తం B-field కూడా దానితో పాటు reverse అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Quick check: at a point on the LEFT of a wire carrying current downward, does B point INTO the page or OUT of it?
tts_sentences.text_hi: Quick check: एक wire पर जिसमें current नीचे की तरफ flow हो रही है, उसके LEFT side के किसी point पर, B page के INTO point करता है या OUT of it?
tts_sentences.text_te: Quick check: కిందికి current carry చేసే wire యొక్క LEFT వైపు ఉన్న ఒక point దగ్గర, B page లోపలికి point చేస్తుందా లేదా బయటికా?

misconception_watch.belief: The B-field strength depends on which way the current flows
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Magnitude B = μ₀I/(2πr) is identical for both cases — only the DIRECTION (CCW vs CW) flips when current reverses.

STATE_5.title: B at a Single Point — Tangent to the Circle

scene_composition.text: Pick one point P — which way does B point?

B at P is TANGENT to this circle
(not radial, not along wire)
B ⊥ plane containing wire and P

tts_sentences.text_en: Pick a single point P next to the wire. Which way does the B-field point exactly there?
tts_sentences.text_hi: wire के पास एक single point P चुनिए। ठीक वहाँ B-field किस तरफ point करता है?
tts_sentences.text_te: wire పక్కన ఒక్క point P తీసుకోండి. సరిగ్గా అక్కడ B-field ఏ దిశలో point చేస్తుంది?

tts_sentences.text_en: The answer: B is tangent to the circle through P. Not radial, not along the wire — tangent.
tts_sentences.text_hi: जवाब यह है: B उस circle पर tangent होता है जो P से गुज़रती है। radial नहीं, wire के along नहीं — बल्कि tangent।
tts_sentences.text_te: సమాధానం ఇది: B అనేది P గుండా వెళ్ళే circle కి tangent గా ఉంటుంది. radial కాదు, wire వెంట కాదు — tangent మాత్రమే.

tts_sentences.text_en: Equivalently, B is perpendicular to the plane that contains both the wire and the point P.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दूसरे शब्दों में, B उस plane के perpendicular है जिसमें wire और point P दोनों हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: మరో విధంగా చెప్పాలంటే, B అనేది wire మరియు point P రెండూ ఉన్న plane కి perpendicular గా ఉంటుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Use the right-hand rule again: thumb up the wire, fingers tell you which way the tangent points at P.
tts_sentences.text_hi: फिर से right-hand rule use करिए: अंगूठे को wire की तरफ ऊपर रखिए, उंगलियाँ बताएंगी कि P पर tangent किस direction में है।
tts_sentences.text_te: మళ్ళీ right-hand rule వాడండి: బొటన వేలిని wire వెంట పైకి పెట్టండి, వేళ్ళు P వద్ద tangent ఏ direction లో ఉందో చెప్తాయి.

STATE_6.title: Bridge to 2D — Where × and · Come From

scene_composition.text: Looking straight DOWN the wire from above

× = INTO the page
(circle going away)

· = OUT of page
(circle coming toward you)

tts_sentences.text_en: Most physics textbooks draw this in 2D. Let me rotate the camera to look straight down at the wire from above.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ज़्यादातर physics textbooks इसे 2D में draw करते हैं। मैं camera को ऊपर से wire की तरफ सीधा rotate करता हूँ।
tts_sentences.text_te: చాలా physics textbooks దీన్ని 2D లో draw చేస్తాయి. నేను camera ని పైనుండి wire వైపు నేరుగా చూసేలా rotate చేస్తాను.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now you see the field circles head-on. On one side of the wire, the circles point INTO the page. On the other side, they point OUT of the page.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आपको field circles सामने से दिख रहे हैं। wire के एक तरफ, circles page के INTO की तरफ point करते हैं। दूसरी तरफ, वे page के OUT of the page की तरफ point करते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీకు field circles నేరుగా కనిపిస్తున్నాయి. wire కి ఒక వైపు, circles INTO the page వైపు point అవుతాయి. మరో వైపు, అవి OUT of the page వైపు point అవుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Textbooks draw a cross — the multiplication sign — for INTO the page. And a dot for OUT of the page.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Textbooks INTO the page के लिए cross — यानी multiplication sign — draw करते हैं। और OUT of the page के लिए dot।
tts_sentences.text_te: Textbooks INTO the page కోసం cross — అంటే multiplication sign — draw చేస్తాయి. మరియు OUT of the page కోసం dot వాడతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: These symbols are NOT showing the current direction. They show the B-field direction at that specific point in 2D.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ये symbols current direction नहीं दिखा रहे। ये 2D में उस specific point पर B-field direction दिखाते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఈ symbols current direction చూపించడం లేదు. ఇవి 2D లో ఆ specific point వద్ద B-field direction చూపిస్తాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now when you see × and · in any diagram, you know they are just the two ends of a 3D circle around a wire — flattened to fit on paper.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब जब भी आप किसी diagram में × और · देखें, आपको पता है कि ये किसी wire के चारों तरफ एक 3D circle के दो सिरे हैं — बस paper पर flat करके दिखाए गए हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీరు ఏ diagram లోనైనా × మరియు · చూసినప్పుడు, అవి ఒక wire చుట్టూ ఉన్న 3D circle యొక్క రెండు చివరలు మాత్రమే అని మీకు తెలుసు — కేవలం paper మీద flat చేసి చూపించబడ్డాయి.

misconception_watch.belief: × and · show which way the current flows
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: × and · in B-field diagrams show the FIELD direction at that point, not the current. Look at what the symbol is labeling.

STATE_7.title: Explore — Rotate, Zoom, Find B at Any Angle

scene_composition.text: Drag to rotate. Scroll to zoom.

Test yourself: at a point ABOVE-RIGHT of the wire,
which way does B point? (Use your right hand)
Same wire, same current — one shape (circles), one rule (right hand).

tts_sentences.text_en: Now you have full control. Drag to rotate. Scroll to zoom. Use the I and r sliders on the right to change current and distance.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आपका पूरा control है। Drag करके rotate करिए। Scroll करके zoom करिए। Current और distance बदलने के लिए right side पर I और r sliders use करिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీకు పూర్తి control ఉంది. Drag చేసి rotate చేయండి. Scroll చేసి zoom చేయండి. Current మరియు distance మార్చడానికి కుడి వైపు I మరియు r sliders వాడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch how the highlighted ring tracks your r-slider, and how all rings brighten when you crank up I.
tts_sentences.text_hi: देखिए कैसे highlighted ring आपके r-slider को track करती है, और जब आप I को बढ़ाते हैं तो सभी rings कैसे brighten हो जाती हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: Highlighted ring మీ r-slider ని ఎలా track చేస్తుందో గమనించండి, మరియు మీరు I ని పెంచినప్పుడు అన్ని rings ఎలా brighten అవుతాయో చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Try to predict B before you read the meter: pick I and r, compute mu-naught I over 2 pi r in your head, then check.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Meter पढ़ने से पहले B predict करने की कोशिश करिए: I और r चुनिए, दिमाग में mu-naught I over 2 pi r compute करिए, फिर check करिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: Meter చదవడానికి ముందు B ని predict చేయడానికి ప్రయత్నించండి: I మరియు r ఎంచుకోండి, మనసులో mu-naught I over 2 pi r compute చేయండి, తర్వాత check చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Same wire, same field shape — circles around the wire. Same rule — your right hand. The numbers just scale.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Same wire, same field shape — wire के चारों ओर circles। Same rule — आपका right hand। Numbers बस scale होते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: Same wire, same field shape — wire చుట్టూ circles. Same rule — మీ right hand. Numbers మాత్రమే scale అవుతాయి.

epic_c_branches.misconception: The B-field points in the same direction as the current — it flows along the wire just like the current does.
trigger_phrases: magnetic field is parallel to current
B in the direction of I

epic_c_branches.misconception: The × and · symbols in textbook diagrams indicate the direction of the current (× = current into page, · = current out of page).
The magnetic field exists only in the 2D plane of the textbook page — the field lines are flat curves on paper, not 3D circles.
Use the left hand for current direction and the right hand for the magnetic force, because that is the natural way to wrap the hand around a vertical wire.

STATE_1.label: Setup — wire and compass at rest
STATE_1.caption: Setup: a vertical wire next to a compass. No field shown yet — the needle points north. Then we'll switch on the current.

STATE_2.label: B circles around the wire (3D)
STATE_2.caption: The B-field forms 3D circles around the wire. Drag to rotate.

STATE_3.label: Case A — I ↑, B counter-clockwise
STATE_3.caption: Case A: current ↑ — B-field circles counter-clockwise (viewed from above). Watch the compass glide in, then deflect.

STATE_4.label: Case B — I ↓, B clockwise
STATE_4.caption: Case B: current ↓ — B-field circles clockwise. Compass approaches the SAME way — but deflects the OPPOSITE direction.

STATE_5.label: B at point P — tangent to the circle
STATE_5.caption: B at P is tangent to the circle through P (not radial, not along wire).

STATE_6.label: Top-down: × and · emerge
STATE_6.caption: Looking straight down: each circle becomes ⊗ (into page) on one side, ⊙ (out) on the other.

STATE_7.label: Free explore — sliders + rotate
STATE_7.caption: Drag sliders to feel B = μ₀I/(2πr). Drag scene to rotate. Scroll to zoom.
STATE_7.formula_overlay:
Right-hand rule:
thumb ↑ I,
fingers ↑ B

tts_sentences.text_en: Step 1: Draw the wire as a vertical line. Mark the current direction with an arrow. Label it I = 5 A.
State Oersted's observation in one line: a current-carrying conductor produces a magnetic field around it.
Step 2: State the right-hand rule. Right thumb along I (upward); curled fingers give B direction.
Draw a small circle around the wire and mark the B-field direction as anti-clockwise viewed from above (Case A).
Step 3: Reverse the current. Now I flows downward. Apply the right-hand rule again — thumb DOWN, fingers curl the opposite way.
Conclusion: when current direction reverses, the B-field direction reverses too. Magnitude is unchanged. Mark this on the answer sheet.
Step 4: Write the magnitude formula. B equals mu-naught I over 2 pi r.
Step 5: Substitute. mu-naught equals 4 pi times 10 to the minus 7. I equals 5. r equals 0.05. B equals 2 times 10 to the minus 5 tesla.

derivation_sequence.writes: Setup: Long straight wire, current I = 5 A flowing vertically (we will examine BOTH directions).
Case A — current upward: by the right-hand rule, B forms concentric circles ANTI-CLOCKWISE around the wire (viewed from above).
Case B — current downward: same right-hand rule with the thumb flipped — B reverses to CLOCKWISE (viewed from above). Magnitude unchanged.
Magnitude: B = μ₀ I / (2π r) = (4π × 10⁻⁷)(5) / (2π × 0.05) = 2 × 10⁻⁵ T (same value for both cases)
Direction at point P: tangent to the circle of radius r through P, sense determined by the right-hand rule for the current direction.

items.criterion: Correctly identifies the setup (long straight wire, current direction, field point at distance r)
States right-hand rule and applies it to upward current (Case A → anti-clockwise B)
Recognises that reversing the current reverses the B-direction (Case B → clockwise) but NOT the magnitude
Writes B = μ₀I/(2πr) and substitutes numerical values correctly with consistent SI units
States the final B with magnitude AND direction (tangent specification, sense from RHR)

shortcuts.title: Quick magnitude in microtesla
shortcuts.rule: B (in μT) ≈ 0.2 × I/r when I is in A and r is in cm. Derived from B = μ₀I/(2πr) with the constants pre-multiplied.

shortcuts.title: Distance doubling halves B
shortcuts.rule: Because B ∝ 1/r, every doubling of r halves B. Useful for ratio questions: 'B at 2r is half B at r.'

shortcuts.title: Current doubling doubles B
shortcuts.rule: Because B ∝ I, every doubling of current doubles B at the same point. Linear in I, inverse in r.

shortcuts.title: RHR cheat for 2D problems
shortcuts.rule: If current ⊙ (out of page): B circles anti-clockwise around it. If current ⊗ (into page): B circles clockwise. Memorize once, apply everywhere.

edge_cases.answer: B → ∞ in the idealization (1/r diverges as r → 0). In practice, the wire has finite radius and the formula B = μ₀I/(2πr) is replaced by B = μ₀Ir/(2πR²) inside, where R is the wire radius.
Use the modified formula B = μ₀I (sin θ₁ + sin θ₂) / (4πr), where θ₁ and θ₂ are angles from the perpendicular to the wire ends. Reduces to μ₀I/(2πr) in the infinite limit (θ₁, θ₂ → 90°).

edge_cases.case: Two parallel wires carrying I₁ and I₂
edge_cases.answer: Use superposition: B at any point is the vector sum of the two individual circular B-fields. If currents are parallel, fields between them oppose; if anti-parallel, fields between them add.

edge_cases.case: Point on the axis of the wire (extended) — not perpendicular to the wire
edge_cases.answer: Trick question. The wire's B-field has zero axial component. B at any point on the wire's axis (extended infinitely) is zero — the right-hand rule has no perpendicular distance r to point at.
Same formula B = μ₀I(t)/(2πr) applies instantaneously, with I(t) = I₀ sin(ωt). The B-field oscillates with the current — useful for understanding electromagnetic waves and induction.
